title: Learner Renderer Kitchen Sink — Sprint 68 Type Coverage

overview.body: Synthetic fixture exercising all thirteen vNext-supported material types across five archetype-matched activities (KS01–KS05).

learning_purpose.body: Validate renderer coverage, type-to-surface mapping, and safe fallback behaviour without modifying GAM or PRISM semantics.

knowledge_summary.body: Each activity maps to one archetype variant. Materials are minimal but schema-valid.

learning_outcomes.statement: Complete table and text learner surfaces where composition applies.

activities.title: Static exposition and verification (understand-explain-check)
activities.learner_task:
1. Read the core text.
2. Study the worked example.
3. Compare your thinking with the sample output.
4. Verify using the checklist.
5. Write a brief summary in your notes.
activities.expected_output: A concise summary demonstrating understanding of the core concept.
activities.activity_preamble: Kitchen-sink KS01 covers text, worked_example, sample_output, and checklist.
activities.reasoning_orientation: Focus on how each material type presents information before you respond.
activities.self_explanation_prompt: How would you explain the difference between exposition and verification materials?

materials.title: Core exposition
materials.body: KS01-M1: **text** material renders as static prose in the explanation beat.

materials.title: Worked pass
materials.body: KS01-M2: **worked_example** shows expert reasoning steps.

materials.title: Sample response
materials.body: KS01-M3: **sample_output** demonstrates quality threshold language.

materials.title: Verification checklist
materials.body:
- Criterion KS01-C1: core concept named
- Criterion KS01-C2: example referenced

Revise if any criterion is missed.

activities.title: Modelling note and prompt set (understand-explain-apply-check)
activities.learner_task:
1. Read the framing text.
2. Review the modelling note.
3. Complete the prompt set responses.
4. Verify with the checklist.
activities.expected_output: Responses addressing each prompt with reference to the modelling note.
activities.activity_preamble: KS02 covers modelling_note and prompt_set materials.
activities.conceptual_contrast_prompt: Contrast static guidance with learner-generated responses.
activities.intellectual_coherence_bridge: Connect KS01 exposition to KS02 application prompts.

materials.title: Framing text
materials.body: KS02-M1: introductory **text** for the apply-check variant.

materials.title: Modelling note
materials.body: KS02-M2: **modelling_note** provides expert modelling guidance without a separate heading duplicate.

materials.title: Application prompts
materials.body:
1. What evidence supports the model?
2. What limitation should a learner note?
3. How would you extend the analysis?

materials.title: Apply checklist
materials.body:
- All three prompts addressed
- Modelling note referenced

activities.title: Scenario and analysis table (analyse-model-analyse-check)
activities.learner_task:
1. Study the worked example.
2. Read the scenario cases.
3. Complete the analysis table.
4. Verify with the checklist.
activities.expected_output: Completed analysis table with evidence-based judgements.
activities.activity_preamble: KS03 covers scenario and analysis_table (static in beats mode).

materials.title: Analytic model
materials.body: KS03-M1: expert analytic pass before independent table completion.

materials.title: Case scenarios
materials.body:
### Case A
Residual spread widens with fitted values.

### Case B
Spread remains constant across the range.

materials.title: Analysis table (reference)
materials.body:
## Analysis grid

| Case | Pattern | Judgement |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Example | Funnel | Likely heteroscedasticity |
| A |  |  |
| B |  |  |

materials.title: Analysis checklist
materials.body:
- Pattern identified
- Judgement justified

activities.title: Decision table practice (apply-model-practise-transfer)
activities.learner_task:
1. Work through the worked example.
2. Apply reasoning to the scenario.
3. Complete the decision table.
4. Verify with the checklist.
5. Identify a transfer context.
activities.expected_output: Completed decision table and transfer reflection.
activities.activity_preamble: KS04 covers decision_table and transfer prompt field.
activities.transfer_or_application_task: Identify another context where the same decision framework applies.

materials.title: Practise model
materials.body: KS04-M1: modelled decision reasoning.

materials.title: Decision scenario
materials.body: A policy team must choose between robust standard errors and variable transformation.

materials.title: Decision table
materials.body:
## Options

| Option | Benefit | Risk | Choice |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Robust SE | Preserves coefficients | Does not fix variance |  |
| Log transform | May stabilise variance | Changes interpretation |  |

materials.title: Practise checklist
materials.body:
- Option benefits named
- Trade-offs acknowledged

activities.title: Evaluation capstone (evaluate-explain-model-practise-transfer)
activities.learner_task:
1. Read the evaluation framing.
2. Compare scenario evidence.
3. Complete the comparison table.
4. Use the response template.
5. Verify with the checklist.
6. Consolidate learning and plan transfer.
activities.expected_output: Structured evaluation with comparison table and written judgement.
activities.activity_preamble: KS05 covers comparison_table, template, consolidation_summary, and transfer_prompt.
activities.argument_structure_hint: State your criterion, evidence, and judgement before recommending an action.
activities.intellectual_coherence_bridge: Connect KS03 analysis to KS05 evaluation.

materials.title: Evaluation framing
materials.body: KS05-M1: evaluation-oriented **text**.

materials.title: Comparison scenario
materials.body: Two remediation approaches are under review for a regression diagnostics report.

materials.title: Comparison table
materials.body:
## Approaches

| Approach | Strength | Limitation |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Residual plots | Visual evidence | Subjective |
| Breusch-Pagan | Formal test | Assumption sensitive |

materials.title: Response template
materials.body:
**Criterion:**

**Evidence:**

**Judgement:**

**Recommendation:**

materials.title: Evaluation checklist
materials.body:
- Criterion stated
- Evidence cited
- Recommendation justified

materials.title: Consolidation
materials.body: Key ideas: residual variance patterns, diagnostic evidence, and remedy trade-offs.

materials.title: Transfer
materials.body: Where else would you apply this evaluation framework in economic analysis?
